calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Approving Final Distribution On Grounds Of Extrinsic Fraud Or Mistake
Off Calendar Sep-18-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Leeds Disston for and with Derrick Tucker (Moving Party/Son of Decedent). Vera Carr (Personal Representative) in Propria Persona.

Derrick Tucker and Vera Carr were sworn in by the Clerk. The Court noted that Mr. Stokes was not present and expressed concern about the Estate being opened and Letters being issued on the basis of facts misrepresented to the Court and possible fraud committed on the Court. Mr. Tucker and Ms. Carr provided testimony under oath with regard to the following: The Decedent having had a will in her home which was kept/left in a drawer; Mr. Stokes having handled/been provided with the Decedent's will; Vera Carr's Sister having been employed by Mr. Stokes as a Legal Secretary; Mr. Stokes and Ms. Carr's Sister's idea on how to proceed with probate/the manner in which Mr. Stokes proceeded with the probate action with false information and in order to "fix" the will to conform to their understanding of the Decedent's intent; Mr. Tucker having been named in said will as the sole Beneficiary to receive the house; The Decedent's desire to save the house for her Grandchildren because Derrick Tucker was in trouble; Mr. Tucker's understanding that the house belongs to him; Mr. Tucker's professed lack of understanding of the document transferring his interest in the home to Ms. Carr; Mr. Tucker's incarceration/crimes committed; The manner in which Mr. Tucker's signature on the document transferring the property was obtained; Ms. Carr's lack of carrying out her obligation to administer the Estate for an eleven year period; etc. At the conclusion of testimony, the Court expressed concern about the possibility of there being uncompensated crime victims and the possible loss of their rights through giving Ms. Carr rather than Mr. Tucker the house. The Court ruled as follows: 1) The Order for Final Distribution filed on July 1, 2003 is vacated. 2) The Order Appointing Ms. Carr as Administrator of the Estate filed on April 11, 2002 is vacated. 3) The Letters of Administration issued to Ms. Carr on May 3, 2002 are void. The Court explained to Mr. Disston that in order to obtain any relief, someone would have to file a new petition in this probate proceeding based on the truth, that it is very likely that neither Ms. Carr or Mr. Tucker would qualify to be appointed as Administrator, and that any new proceedings would have to account for the will. The Court also suggested that a future Administrator or the Court itself would likely want to investigate the conduct of Mr. Stokes and Vera Carr in this matter. In addition, the Court stated that someone may need to determine if Derrick Tucker has outstanding restitution obligations and, if so, whether this Court should do anything about them.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Order Approving Final Distribution On Grounds Of Extrinsic Fraud Or Mistake
Off Calendar Aug-21-2013 Continued To Sep-18-13 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Leeds Disston for Derrick Tucker.

Mr. Disston stated that he has a Proof of Service, that service was done by mail on Ms. Carr, Mr. Stokes and Mr. Tucker on July 19, 2013 and that he will file it. Mr. Disston also requested a date to bring Ms. Carr and Mr. Tucker in to Court to address the issues raised in the tentative ruling. The Court suggested that Mr. Stokes also be present. The Motion to Set Aside Order Approving Final Distribution on Grounds of Extrinsic Fraud or Mistake was continued to September 18, 2013 at 2:00 p.m., in Dept. 204. The Court directed Counsel to serve notice of the new hearing date on Ms. Carr and Mr. Tucker.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. (Not Reported)
